title: Health & Personal Care (HPC) products or Industrial Chemicals | Regulatory & Product Safety Manager/Regulatory Affairs Specialist |5 Days | West
description:
Regulatory & Product Safety Manager |5 Days | West

Employment Type: Full-Time

Industry: Chemicals / Manufacturing / Regulatory Affairs

Working Days: 5-day work week (Office-based)

Job Description

We are looking for an experienced Regulatory & Product Safety Manager to lead global regulatory compliance, product safety, and stewardship activities across Home & Personal Care, Industrial Chemicals, and Pharmaceutical-related applications.

This role ensures regulatory readiness, market access, and risk mitigation, while working closely with R&D, Quality, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Commercial teams.

Key Responsibilities

Regulatory Compliance & Strategy

  • Ensure compliance with global chemical regulations (e.g. REACH, TSCA, China MEE, K-REACH, UK-REACH, CEPA, AICIS)
  • Lead regulatory submissions, registrations, notifications, and dossier preparation
  • Monitor regulatory developments and assess impact on products and market access
  • Develop and implement regulatory strategies to support new product launches

Product Safety & Risk Assessment

  • Prepare and review Safety Data Sheets (SDS), exposure scenarios, and GHS/CLP labelling
  • Conduct toxicological and ecotoxicological assessments using OECD guidelines
  • Ensure compliance with consumer and industrial safety standards (EU Cosmetics, US CFR, ASEAN Cosmetics, IFRA, etc.)

Product Stewardship & Lifecycle Management

  • Drive product stewardship programmes from R&D to end-of-life
  • Support sustainability initiatives (safer chemistry, biodegradability, VOC reduction)
  • Evaluate regulatory impact during formulation changes and raw material substitutions
  • Maintain awareness of restricted and prohibited substances

Cross-Functional & External Engagement

  • Advise R&D on regulatory feasibility during formulation development
  • Support Sales and Marketing with regulatory documentation and product claims
  • Work with Manufacturing and Supply Chain on site compliance and transport regulations
  • Liaise with regulators, consultants, auditors, and industry associations

Systems, Documentation & Compliance

  • Maintain regulatory databases, SDS libraries, and compliance records
  • Oversee SDS and labelling systems (e.g. SAP EHS, Sphera, Chemwatch)
  • Support customer audits, regulatory training, and internal compliance reviews

Job Requirements

Qualifications

  • Master’s or Doctorate in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Toxicology, Regulatory Affairs, or Environmental Science

Experience

  • Minimum 8years of experience in regulatory affairs, product safety, or product stewardship
  • Proven experience in HPC, industrial chemicals, or pharmaceutical-related products
  • Strong knowledge of international chemical regulations and product registrations
  • Experience in toxicology and ecotoxicology assessments

Skills & Competencies

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
  • Proficient in SDS authoring tools, regulatory databases, and ERP systems
  • Ability to manage multiple projects in a global environment

Additional Perks

  • Senior-level position with regional/global exposure
  • Competitive salary commensurate with experience
